Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂前
English: February 9 Inner Temple Gate

Japanese: ‥‥この前とちがって、 なんか騒がしいな‥‥
English: Things have certainly become a lot livelier than before.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥捜査が 始まったようね。
English: They must have commenced with the investigation.

Japanese: あっ‥‥なるほどくん！
English: Oh! ...Mr. Nick!

Japanese: （こ、この声は‥‥）
English: (Th-That voice...)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん！
English: Pearls!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥ なるほどくううううん！
English: ...Mr. Nick... Mr. Niiick!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥やっぱり、あの夜は‥‥ ここにいたんだ。
English: So you were here... ever since that night...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥もう、さみしくて 死んでしまうかと思いました。
English: I was so lonely, I thought I was going to die!

Japanese: 朝、起きてみると、 おぼろ橋はなくなっていて‥‥
English: When I woke up in the morning and saw that Dusky Bridge was gone...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥ひとりぼっちで。 I...
English: I realized I was all alone.

Japanese: （“ひとりぼっち”‥‥？）
English: (She was all alone...?)

Japanese: それは大変だったわね、 おじょうちゃん。
English: It must have been very trying for you, little girl.

Japanese: あ‥‥！ あなたは‥‥
English: Ah! You're...

Japanese: 狩魔　冥。‥‥天才検事よ。 私が来たからには、もう安心‥‥
English: I am Franziska von Karma, the Prodigy. There's no need to worry now that I'm here.

Japanese: ‥‥１年前、真宵さまを いじめた、検事さまですね。
English: You're the prosecutor who was so mean to Mystic Maya last year.
Well...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥キライですっ。
English: I... I don't like you. You're nothing but a little girl without your whip!

Japanese: 真宵さまは悪くなかったのに‥‥ わたくし、ユルしませんっ！
English: Mystic Maya didn't do anything wrong, but you were so mean! I'll never forgive you!

Japanese: （‥‥そうとう 効いてるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like Pearls's words are getting under her skin...)

Japanese: いててててッ！ モノも言わずに、たたくなよ！
English: Yeowwww! Why are you whipping me!? I didn't even say anything!

Japanese: うれしそうなカオ、するなッ！
English: You didn't have to. The smile on your lips gave you away!

Japanese: そ‥‥それより、春美ちゃん。
English: A-Anyway... Pearls...?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは‥‥ いっしょじゃないの？
English: Weren't you with Maya?
Ah!

Japanese: 申しわけありません！ ゼンブ‥‥わたくしのせいですっ！
English: I'm sorry! It's... It's all my fault!

Japanese: は‥‥春美ちゃん‥‥？
English: Huh!? What are you talking about, Pearls?

Japanese: あああああああああああああん！ まよいさまああああああああっ！
English: Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaah! Mystic Mayaaaaaaa!

Japanese: あ！　‥‥春美ちゃん！ （走って行っちまった‥‥）
English: Hey, wait! Pearls! (She just ran off!)

Japanese: いててててッ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: ‥‥女の子を泣かせるなんて‥‥ サイテイね、成歩堂　龍一！
English: That was cruel, Phoenix Wright. To make a little girl cry like that is inexcusable!

Japanese: （ど‥‥どういうこと、だ？ イヤな胸騒ぎがする‥‥）
English: (What was that all about? Pearls acting like that... It's giving me the creeps.)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥もう一度！ よく調べなおしてみよう！）
English: (I guess I'd better take another look around since I've finally got a chance!)

Japanese: （‥‥どことなく、２日前と フンイキが変わった気がするし）
English: (Especially since something about this place seems different from two days ago.)

Japanese: ‥‥自分は、どうすれば いいッスかねえ‥‥
English: I dunno... Should I... Or shouldn't I...?

Japanese: （‥‥風に乗って、どこからか ヒトリゴトが聞こえてくる）
English: (Sound like someone's talking to himself, but where's that coming from?)

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、アレは 水洗いしておいたほうが‥‥
English: Yeah. I guess I better wash that off. That's what I'll do.

Japanese: ‥‥イヤ。でも、刑事として さすがにそれは‥‥
English: No, but I can't do that. I'm a detective...

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。悩むッス。 小さなムネが張り裂けそうッス‥‥
English: ...Argh! My brain! This is driving me nuts!

Japanese: ぎゃはああッ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: どうしたの？　ヒゲ。
English: Is something troubling you, Scruffy?

Japanese: い。いきなりピシリと やらないでほしいッス！
English: P-Please don't whip me like that all of a sudden, sir!

Japanese: アイサツがわりに、肩をポンと たたいただけよ。‥‥ムチで。
English: It was merely a simple greeting. A friendly tap on the shoulder... with my whip.

Japanese: どうしたんですか？ 悩んでいるそうですね。
English: So what's up, Gumshoe? Is something on your mind?

Japanese: どどどど、どーして 知ってるッスかッ！
English: H-H-How did you know, pal!?

Japanese: “アレ”を水洗いするとか しないとか。
English: Perhaps you were trying to decided if you wanted to wash "that" off or not...?

Japanese: どどどど、どーして 知ってるッスかッ！
English: H-H-How did you know, sir!?

Japanese: ‥‥あッ！　そッス！
English: Oh! Yeah! I nearly forgot.

Japanese: アンタ、あの小さなトビラは 入っちゃダメッスよ！
English: You can't go through that little gate there, OK?

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。あの《立入禁止》の 立て札があるトビラですか？
English: Um... You mean the one that says "No Entry" on it?

Japanese: じ、自分はちゃんと、 しゃがんで入ったッスから！
English: I... um... kinda already snuck in there!

Japanese: （‥‥そんなトコで、とんちを きかせてどうするんだよ‥‥）
English: (Is he trying to be clever or something...?)

Japanese: あのトビラの向こうには 何があるの？　ヒゲ。
English: So what's behind there, Scruffy?

Japanese: な、な、な、な、中庭ふうの、 ‥‥いわゆる、中庭ッス。
English: Huh? A kind of... garden-like garden.

Japanese: なかにわ‥‥？
English: A garden?

Japanese: と、とにかく、捜査中ッスから。 ゼーッタイ、来ちゃダメッスよ！
English: Yeah. Anyway, it's under investigation right now, so keep out!

Japanese: これは、《命令》‥‥というより、 むしろ《おねがい》ッス！
English: I'm not telling you, I'm asking you, pal! You got me?

Japanese: （逆じゃないか？　ふつう‥‥？）
English: (Isn't it normally the other way around?)

Japanese: じゃッ！
English: Well, see you later!

Japanese: ‥‥それじゃ、中庭に行くわ。 成歩堂　龍一。
English: So... shall we adjourn to this garden, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: （そりゃ、そうだよなあ）
English: (Of course! What else would we do?)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事が、何を 水洗いするのか、知りたいし）
English: (I really want to know what Gumshoe was thinking of washing off!)

Japanese: おぼろ橋が見える。 よく見ると、意外に長い。
English: You can see Dusky Bridge from here. It's an unusually long bridge, isn't it?

Japanese: ２０メートル近くはあるようね。
English: I would say it's about 20 yards.

Japanese: これじゃあ、ワイヤーを伝って 向こう岸にわたるのはムリだな。
English: I guess the gorge is too wide to cross with a piece of rope or wire, huh?

Japanese: つまり、事件当夜から今日まで、 ここに出入りできた人間は、ない。
English: Therefore, no one has crossed the gorge in either direction since the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥トーゼンね。成歩堂　龍一。
English: A simple, yet sound deduction, wouldn't you say, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: なんだよ。いちいちフルネームで 呼ばないでほしいな。
English: What!? And would you stop calling me by my full name like that?

Japanese: かつて、ここは港町として 栄えていたのかしら？
English: Was this once a prosperous port?

Japanese: ‥‥みなとまち？
English: A port...?

Japanese: あそこにあるのは、フネをつなぎ とめるための金具でしょう？
English: Yes. That metal hook is a mooring post for boats, you know.

Japanese: ああ。あれは、おぼろ橋を補強する ワイヤーがかかっていたんだよ。
English: Oh, that. That's an anchor for one of the wires that used to hold up the bridge.

Japanese: 橋が燃えたときに、 切れちゃったみたいだけど。
English: I think the wire that was tied to this one probably snapped when the bridge burned down.

Japanese: （‥‥こんなさみしい山の中に 港町が栄えるワケないだろうが）
English: (I mean, there's no way there was ever a port all the way up here...)

Japanese: うるさいッ！　成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: Don't get smart with me, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 何も言ってないよ！
English: But I didn't say anything!

Japanese: お地蔵さまがズラリと 並んでいる。
English: Look at all the Buddha statues lined up along this path.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: ジューショクさまには、 こんなに姉妹がいたのかしら？
English: Do you think this is how many siblings the head nun has?

Japanese: あとで、本人に聞いてみたら いいんじゃないかな。
English: Why don't you ask her the next time you see her?

Japanese: ‥‥そうしてみるわ。
English: ...You know what? I think I will.

Japanese: （ひっぱたかれるぞ、たぶん）
English: (And she'll probably slap you for prying into her private affairs.)

Japanese: （あの焼却炉‥‥あやしいニオイが ただよっているな）
English: (There's a weird smell coming from that incinerator.)

Japanese: （これ見よがしに、フタも 開きっぱなしになってるし‥‥）
English: (The door is also open, almost like it's begging me to look inside...)

Japanese: ‥‥どれどれ。 いったい、どんな手がかりが‥‥
English: Well, well. Let's see what stunning clue is concealed in here, shall we?

Japanese: 信じられない。カラッポだ。
English: Huh? It's empty!

Japanese: アマいわね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: How naïve of you, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: でも‥‥ちょっと、 気になるんだよ。‥‥狩魔　冥。
English: But it's a bit strange, don't you think? ...Franziska von Karma?

Japanese: （ぼくが最初に見たときは、 たしか‥‥）
English: (I seem to remember...)

Japanese: （あの焼却炉には、 雪がつもっていた）
English: (...there was snow on this incinerator the first time I saw it.)

Japanese: （やっぱり！　だれかが、 何かを燃やしたんだ‥‥）
English: (In other words, someone's been using it to burn something recently...)

Japanese: ‥‥ひとつ、いいかしら？ 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Listen, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ヒトをフルネームで呼ばないで！ なんか、気にさわるわ。
English: It's impertinent to call people by their full name!

Japanese: ‥‥きみのマネをしただけだぞ。
English: I was only copying you.

Japanese: ‥‥ここが《奥の院》ね。
English: This must be the door that leads to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: 事件当夜、綾里　真宵が 修行をしていたという‥‥
English: Where Maya Fey was training on the night of the murder...

Japanese: ああ。この奥に《修験堂》と 呼ばれる庵があるんだ。
English: Yes. There's a solitary room there called the Training Hall.

Japanese: あなたも修行が必要みたいね、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: You could do with some training yourself, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: YEOWWW!

Japanese: ノリが悪いわ。
English: You're completely out of shape.

Japanese: （ううううう‥‥ たまんないな、まったく）
English: (Nngh... I don't know how much more of this woman I can take!)

Japanese: 《奥の院》と書かれている。
English: The sign says "Inner Temple".

Japanese: 私には、そうは読めないわ。
English: I don't know how you're reading it, but it's illegible to me.

Japanese: 正直なところ、ぼくにも読めない。
English: Actually, I can't read it either.

Japanese: ‥‥キメつけはよくないわね、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Feigning comprehension isn't a very good habit to have, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （そんなコトで 勝ちほこられてもなあ）
English: (She looks really happy to have finally caught me on something...)

Japanese: 中庭に通じる、 小さなトビラがある。
English: There's a little gate that leads into the garden.

Japanese: 横の立て札には《立入禁止》 って書いてあるな。
English: It says "No Entry" on the sign, though.

Japanese: 何してるの、成歩堂　龍一。 入るわよ。
English: Come now, Phoenix Wright! What are you doing? Let's go in.

Japanese: トビラは半開き。 ‥‥なんの問題もないわ。
English: It's half open anyway. What harm could come of it?

Japanese: でも、《立入禁止》だぞ。
English: But it says, "No Entry".

Japanese: 《イヤよイヤよも好きのうち》 ‥‥この国では、そう言うそうね。
English: Don't you Americans enjoy doing whatever you like and then simply say, "Whatever!?"

Japanese: ‥‥まったくカンケイないよ。 （ダレから教わったんだ‥‥）
English: That's got nothing to do with anything! (And who told you that!?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂内
English: February 9 Inner Temple Training Hall

Japanese: どうしたのかしら？ ダマりこんで。
English: What's wrong? Why are you so quiet?

Japanese: ここで修行していた はずなんだよ。‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya... She was supposed to be in here training.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 姿は見えないわね。
English: Yet, it appears she's nowhere to be seen.

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ？ あの妙な《錠》は‥‥？）
English: (What's that...? That strange lock...?)

Japanese: （あんなの、２日前にはなかった。 それに‥‥）
English: (It wasn't there two days ago.)

Japanese: （この部屋‥‥どうも、妙な フンイキがただよっている‥‥）
English: (This whole room is really giving off some strange vibes...)

Japanese: ずいぶん、おそかったじゃねえか。
English: What took you so long?

Japanese: 待ちくたびれちまったぜ。 ‥‥まるほどう‥‥
English: I thought even you'd manage to get here faster than this, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！ 来てたんですか‥‥
English: P-Prosecutor Godot! I didn't know you were here.

Japanese: “検事”‥‥ですって？
English: Prosecutor?

Japanese: ゴドー検事！　今日の法廷‥‥ どうして行かなかったんですか！
English: How come you didn't show up at the trial today?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　それは アンタも同じだって聞いたぜ。
English: Ha...! I could ask you the exact same question.

Japanese: え！　ぼ、ぼくは‥‥その。 カゼをひいちゃったから‥‥
English: Huh? But I was... I had a cold, so...

Japanese: ‥‥オレには、もう少し 大事な用があったのさ。
English: I had something slightly more important than a common cold to deal with.

Japanese: アンタには、一生 リカイできないだろうが、な。
English: The importance of which is something you have no hope of ever understanding.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、読めたわ。
English: Enough! I believe I have the measure of you.

Japanese: キサマね。神聖なる法廷を すっぽかした、最低の検事！
English: You are the very worst kind of prosecutor. What could be more important than a trial?

Japanese: ダレだい？　この、 じゃじゃ馬ムスメは。
English: Who's the wild mare, Trite?

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、あなたの かわりをつとめた、狩魔検事です。
English: This is Ms. von Karma. She was the acting prosecutor in your absence today.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そいつは ゴクロウだったなァ。
English: Ha...! Well, I guess I owe you one then.

Japanese: おじょうちゃんは、もういいぜ。 ‥‥これからは、オトナの時間だ。
English: But you can go now, princess. It's time for the big boys to take the reins.

Japanese: な、何を勝手なコトを！ この事件は、私の‥‥
English: Just who do you think you are!? This case is my...

Japanese: おしゃべりなオンナは 好きじゃねえ‥‥。
English: Hey, Filly. Know your role, and shut your mouth. I can't stand women like you.

Japanese: もう一度だけ、言う。 ‥‥アンタ、帰っていいぜ。
English: I'm only going to say this once, Lady von Whippingberg. Go home!

Japanese: なるほどおおおッ！
English: Phoeeenix Wriiight!

Japanese: だから、なんでぼくを たたくんだよ！
English: Wh-What are you hitting me for!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ムチじゃァ まだ、足りねえぜ。まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! You deserve more cracks of the whip than that, Trite.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: まだ、アンタはわかっていねえ。
English: You still don't get it, do you?

Japanese: 自分が、取り返しのつかないコトを したかもしれない、ってなァ‥‥
English: You don't realize that you've set something in motion that you'll never be able to undo.

Japanese: （ご、ゴドー検事‥‥ なんか、いつもとちがうぞ）
English: (There's something different about Godot today...)

Japanese: （マスクの裏から‥‥ 強烈なチカラを感じる！）
English: (I'm getting such a strong sense of... something from behind that mask of his.)

Japanese: （これは‥‥《怒り》と‥‥ そして‥‥）
English: (Is it anger? Or...)
(Is it... sorrow?)

Japanese: さてと。
English: So.

Japanese: そろそろシゴトに 戻るとするぜ。
English: I should be getting back to work.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！
English: Wait, Godot!

Japanese: まちがいないんですか？ ‥‥真宵ちゃんのこと。
English: How sure about Maya are you?

Japanese: オレに、まちがいはねえ。
English: There's no doubt in my mind.

Japanese: この《修験洞》の中にいる‥‥ それしか考えようがねえのさ。
English: She's in the Sacred Cavern somewhere. It's the only place she could be.

Japanese: まあ、せいぜい 祈っておくことだ。
English: You'd better start praying.

Japanese: アンタの親友が、さっさと 被告人を連れてくるコトを。
English: You'd better pray that friend of yours brings the accused back here soon.

Japanese: （御剣‥‥か）
English: (Edgeworth?)

Japanese: ‥‥いいか、まるほどう。 これだけは言っておく。
English: Listen up, Trite. There's only one thing I want to say to you before I go.

Japanese: オレは決して、 アンタを認めねえぜ。
English: I'll never accept you. Never.

Japanese: ‥‥トモダチは選んだほうが いいわね。成歩堂　龍一。
English: You should choose your friends more carefully, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: みんな、そう言うよ。
English: That's what everyone says.

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥）
English: (It looks like I don't have much of a choice...)

Japanese: （ぼくは、ぼくにできるコトを するしかない、か）
English: (I guess I'll just have to do what I can for now.)

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 この黄色いポスターは何かしら？
English: What do you think this yellowish poster is, Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: ああ。それは《掛け軸》だよ。 描かれている女のヒトは‥‥‥‥
English: It's a scroll, not a poster. It's a picture of a woman who's actually...

Japanese: ‥‥“女のヒト”と言われても‥‥ 私には、何も見えないわ。
English: A woman? I don't see any woman here.

Japanese: （この部屋に来たときから、どうも 妙なムードがただよっていた）
English: (There's a different atmosphere in this room since the last time I was here.)

Japanese: （ただよっていたのは、ムード じゃない！　‥‥ニオイだ！）
English: (No... It's not a different atmosphere, it's a different smell!)

Japanese: ‥‥カレー‥‥
English: It's gravy!

Japanese: ？　どうしたの、成歩堂　龍一。
English: ...What's the matter?

Japanese: この掛け軸‥‥。カレーが ぶちまけられているんだよ。
English: This scroll... It's been completely covered in gravy!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥この部屋は、食欲を そそるニオイがたちこめているわ。
English: Ah. Yes. There is a very appetizing smell in the air.

Japanese: でも‥‥なぜ？ カレーはインドの食文化よ。
English: But gravy is a type of sauce.

Japanese: この国では、インドのカレーを 画材として使用するのかしら？
English: So when you run out of paint, you Americans use gravy as a substitute, I see.

Japanese: どの国でも、使用しないと思うよ。 ‥‥まず、ニオうし。
English: No! No one does that! For starters, it stinks!

Japanese: （事件があった夜‥‥みんなで 食べたカレー‥‥だよな、これ）
English: (This must be the gravy that we had with the roast on the night of the incident.)

Japanese: （いったい‥‥だれが、なぜ？ 掛け軸に、こんなコトを‥‥）
English: (But why would anyone do this? Why this scroll?)

Japanese: 《掛け軸》のデータを修正した。 詳細で表示を切り替えられる。
English: Hanging Scroll updated. Touch to see before and after view under the Check screen.

Japanese: （倉院流霊媒道の家元、 綾里　舞子が描かれた、掛け軸）
English: (This scroll shows a picture of Misty Fey, Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.)

Japanese: （そこに、なぜか‥‥ カレーがぶちまけられている）
English: (But why would anyone cover it in gravy?)

Japanese: ‥‥これじゃあ、何が 描いてあるのかわからないわ。
English: I can't make out what's drawn on this scroll at all.

Japanese: この国の芸術家のやるコトは、 リカイできないわね。
English: I'll never understand you Americans and your so-called artists...

Japanese: （ゲージュツ、ねえ‥‥）
English: (Artists? Um, yeah...)

Japanese: 古ぼけたタンスだ。
English: It's an antique dresser.

Japanese: 勝手に開けちゃダメよ、 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Don't you dare open it, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （なんか、真宵ちゃんに なった気分だな‥‥）
English: (Now I know how Maya feels when I tell her not to touch things...)

Japanese: なんだ。修験者の装束しか 入っていないわ。
English: What a pity. It's full of nothing but old clothes for the acolytes.

Japanese: ‥‥勝手に開けちゃ ダメなんじゃなかったのかよ。
English: I thought we weren't opening it!

Japanese: 私は検事局の人間なのよ。 ナニをやってもユルされるの。
English: I'm from the Prosecutor's Office. I can do anything.

Japanese: （‥‥ショーゲキ的な発言だな）
English: (Yeah, you can do anything... Except stand up to a 9 year old girl.)

Japanese: ‥‥これは何かしら？ 葉桜院にもあったけど。
English: What is this? There was one in the Main Hall as well.

Japanese: “火ばち”っていうんだ。 ストーブみたいなもの、かな。
English: It's a kind of hearth. It's called a "hibachi".

Japanese: へえ、おもしろいわ。
English: Really? How interesting.

Japanese: ちょっと、火をつけてみて。 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Light it for me, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ぼく、使い方は知らないんだ。
English: I-I don't know how...

Japanese: いてててッ！
English: Ouch!

Japanese: キサマのようなクサったワカゾウが 文化の火に水をかけるのだッ！
English: Dim-witted morons like you are to blame for the cultural decay that blots our future!

Japanese: ‥‥今度、ベンキョウしておくよ。 （“文化”と言われると弱い）
English: I-I'll look into it and learn! (*sniffle* Now even Maya is more cultured than me...)

Japanese: このトビラ‥‥
English: Hmm... This door...

Japanese: ２日前に来たときは、こんな 妙な《錠》は、かかってなかった。
English: When I was here two days ago, that weird lock wasn't on it.

Japanese: まるで、ほら穴を守っているかの ようね。‥‥この、クサリ。
English: Those chains... It's almost as if they're guarding something inside that cavern.

Japanese: こんなフシギな《錠》‥‥ 見たことがないわ。
English: I've never seen a lock quite like this before.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくには、見おぼえがある。 この《クサリ》と《錠》‥‥）
English: (I have. I've seen locks and chains just like this before.)

Japanese: （ココロの暗闇を守る 《サイコ・ロック》‥‥で）
English: (They look just like the ones that guard a person's secrets during a Psyche-Lock.)

Japanese: （この《錠》も、何かを かくしているのだろうか？）
English: (I wonder if this lock is guarding something, too.)

Japanese: （《ほら穴》という 暗闇の向こうに‥‥）
English: (Dark secrets in a dark cavern...)

Japanese: せんべいブトンが たたまれている。
English: Look at those wafer-thin mattresses!

Japanese: ちょっと待ちなさい、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: I'm sorry, Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: 何かしら？ 《せんべいブトン》って。
English: "Wafer-thin"?

Japanese: ウスくてペッタリした、 おせんべいみたいなフトンだよ。
English: Yeah, you know. Thin like a wafer. In other words, uncomfortable.

Japanese: この国じゃ、そう言うの。
English: That's how we describe things like these mattresses.

Japanese: アメリカで言えば‥‥ 《ビスケットタオルケット》かな。
English: I guess you must say, "wafer- thick" or something back in Germany?

Japanese: そ、そうなの。 ‥‥勉強になったわ。
English: Not about these mattresses. Certainly not!

Japanese: 《大事な用》があったんですよね？ だから、法廷に行かなかった。
English: You had some important business and that's why you weren't in court, huh?

Japanese: その《大事な用》って‥‥ なんだったんですか？
English: So what was it?

Japanese: 前に一度だけ、言ったことがある。 おぼえているかい‥‥？
English: I've told you once before, but perhaps you don't remember.

Japanese: オレは、アンタと 戦わなけりゃならねえ。
English: I've returned from the depths of Hell...

Japanese: そのために‥‥ 地獄から戻ってきたのさ。
English: To do battle with you.

Japanese: ‥‥オレはなァ。 一度、死んだオトコ、なんだよ。
English: You see, Trite... I've experienced something most have not. Death.
You... died?

Japanese: 死人が地獄からよみがえるには‥‥ くだらねえ手つづきが必要なのさ。
English: Of course, being extradited from Hell is a tedious affair.

Japanese: 定期的な身体機能の精密検査、 体内物質の補給・調整‥‥
English: The meticulous regeneration and adjustment of all your internal organs is...

Japanese: めんどくせえ現代医学にすがって、 みっともなく生きながらえる‥‥
English: Well... Let's just say modern medicine allows us all to live to a ripe old age.

Japanese: それが‥‥今の、このオレだ。
English: Even someone like me.

Japanese: そ、それじゃあ‥‥ その、マスクも‥‥？
English: So... you mean... That mask you wear is...?

Japanese: この、みにくいシロモノかい？ シャレでつけてるワケじゃねえぜ。
English: This ugly device? I promise it's not a fashion statement, my unenlightened friend.

Japanese: コイツがなきゃ、オレはアンタの マヌケヅラを見るコトもできねえ。
English: Without this, I can't see your frequently dumbfounded face.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥
English: I... I didn't know...

Japanese: ‥‥オレの目は、とっくに イカれちまっているのさ。
English: My eyesight is pretty messed up.

Japanese: こんなデカいメガネをブラ下げて いても‥‥完全には見えねえ。
English: Even with these huge goggles on my head, I still can't see everything.

Japanese: まあ‥‥、このポンコツな 身体を点検していたワケだ。
English: Still, I keep this worn-out piece of junk of a body going with regular servicing.

Japanese: いったい‥‥
English: I'm sorry, but...

Japanese: 何があったというの？ 《一度、死んだ》‥‥などと‥‥
English: ...you say you experienced death? How is that possible? What happened...?

Japanese: それは、コイツに聞いてくれよ。
English: Why don't you ask him?

Japanese: え‥‥！ ぼ‥‥ぼく‥‥ですか！
English: Huh? M-Me!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうさ、まるほどう。 アンタは、知っているハズだ。
English: Yeah, Trite. You. You should know all about it.

Japanese: このオレが‥‥いつ。 そして、だれに“殺されたのか”？
English: You know when my life ended... And who ended it for me.

Japanese: な、成歩堂　龍一ッ！ ‥‥そうなの‥‥？
English: W-Well, Phoenix Wright! Do you!?

Japanese: そ、そんなこと言われても‥‥
English: I-I don't know what he's talking about...

Japanese: （‥‥アタマの中に、 何かが引っかかっていた‥‥）
English: (Although... To be honest...)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事が “殺された”事件‥‥）
English: (I do feel a little tug at the corner of my memory...)

Japanese: （ぼくは‥‥ それを、“知っている”‥‥）
English: (I think I do know about how Godot was "killed"...)

Japanese: いずれ‥‥ ハッキリするだろうさ。
English: It will all become clear in due time.

Japanese: ‥‥何もかも、な。 そうだろう？　まるほどう‥‥
English: Isn't that right, Trite?

Japanese: ぼくが‥‥“取り返しの つかないこと”をした‥‥
English: Earlier, you said I've done something I can never undo.

Japanese: さっき、そう言いましたね。 ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What did you mean by that exactly?

Japanese: オンボロ橋の、こちら側‥‥ 奥の院は、《陸の孤島》だ。
English: The Inner Temple here on this side of Dusky Bridge is an isolated island.

Japanese: （それは、ビキニさんも 言ってたな‥‥）
English: (That's what Sister Bikini said, too.)

Japanese: なあ、アンタ。知ってるかい？
English: And I wonder, Trite...

Japanese: 今日、こっちにわたってきた 警官たちが、何をしているか‥‥
English: Do you know what the police are doing here today?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: ...What do you mean?

Japanese: 連中、必死で探しているのさ。
English: They're searching...

Japanese: 消えちまった修験者の 痕跡を‥‥、な。
English: Searching for any trace of an acolyte who went missing.
M-Missing?

Japanese: ‥‥捜索隊を代表して、 これだけは断言してやろう。
English: As leader of the search party, I can tell you this with absolute certainty...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、オンボロ橋の こちら側には、存在しねえ。
English: Maya Fey is not on this side of Dusky Bridge.
Huh?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな、バカな！ だって‥‥あの晩、彼女は‥‥
English: B-But that's impossible! She came here that night and...

Japanese: 同じコトを、二度は言わねえ。 可能性は、ゼロ‥‥だ。
English: I won't say it again. The chances of her being here are nil.

Japanese: ただ‥‥１カ所をのぞいて、な。
English: Excluding of course, one very unique place...
(One? Where is that?)

Japanese: 目の前にある、 この《修験洞》の中さ。
English: The Sacred Cavern, the entrance of which we are standing at right now.

Japanese: 捜索隊が調べていないのは、 あとは、この中だけだ。
English: This is the only place that the search party has yet to explore.

Japanese: （‥‥この、ほら穴か‥‥）
English: (So they haven't searched that cavern yet, huh...)

Japanese: ただ‥‥修験洞内の温度は、 氷点下にいたることもある。
English: Be aware, though, that the temperature inside frequently falls below freezing.

Japanese: もし‥‥この中に 綾里　真宵がいたとしても‥‥
English: Even if she was in there...

Japanese: ‥‥正直、生存の可能性は 高いとは言えないだろうぜ。
English: ...the chances of her being found alive are slim at best.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥！
English: N-No!

Japanese: アンタは、綾里　真宵を 見殺しにしたことになるのさ。
English: Which means, Trite, that you sent Maya Fey to her death!

Japanese: バカなッ！
English: You fool!

Japanese: それならば‥‥なぜ！ さっさと捜査をしないの！
English: If that's the situation, why aren't you in there with a search party right now!?

Japanese: アンタにも、見えるだろう？ そこにブラ下がっている《錠》が。
English: You must have blinders on, my equestrian angel. Don't you see the big lock and chains?

Japanese: 《からくり錠》だ。 今、解除の手配をしている。
English: It's a trick lock. I'm making preparations to open it as we speak.

Japanese: ‥‥どうだ？　まるほどう。
English: Well, Trite?

Japanese: アンタのせいで‥‥またしても 女がひとり、死ぬ。
English: Once again, a woman dies because of you.

Japanese: ま‥‥“またしても”‥‥？ な、何を言い出すんだ！
English: "Once again"!? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 忘れたとは言わせねえ。 ‥‥まるほどう‥‥
English: Don't tell me you've forgotten...?

Japanese: 今から、３年前だ。
English: It was only two years ago, after all...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタのせいで、 死んだ女がいただろう？
English: ...when the last unfortunate woman died because of you.

Japanese: （ぼ‥‥ぼくのせいで‥‥？）
English: (Because of me...?)

Japanese: 綾里　真宵の、姉‥‥ そう。綾里　千尋さ！
English: And do you know who that was? It was Maya Fey's sister! That's right! Mia Fey!

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋‥‥さん‥‥？
English: M-Mia!?

Japanese: 彼女は、アンタが殺したんだ！
English: You killed her!

Japanese: ち‥‥ちがうッ！
English: N-No, that's not how it was!

Japanese: ‥‥なんなの？　この《錠》は。
English: What is this peculiar-looking lock?

Japanese: こんなもの、さっさと コワしてしまえば‥‥
English: Why don't we just break it open? It would be a simple matter of...

Japanese: そうは行かねえのさ。
English: I'm afraid we can't do that.

Japanese: このあたりは、ムカシから 地震の多い土地がらでな‥‥
English: This area has always been prone to earthquakes.

Japanese: たび重なる振動で、《修験洞》の 地盤は、もうガタガタなんだ。
English: The repeated tremors have weakened the foundations of the Training Hall.

Japanese: カギを破壊するために よけいなショックを与えたら‥‥
English: Any excessive force used to break the lock open would...

Japanese: 《奥の院》自体が、ほら穴ごと、 崩れちまうだろうぜ。
English: Well, let's just say the Inner Temple and the Sacred Cavern would be a thing of the past.

Japanese: じゃあ！　さっさと 《錠》を開けたらどうなの！
English: Very well. Then dare I suggest the obvious solution of opening it with the key?

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！
コイツは、カギ穴のない 《からくり錠》なのさ。
English: Sure. Just show me where the keyhole is on this trick lock.

Japanese: カギ穴が‥‥ない？
English: Th-There's no keyhole!?

Japanese: 一種のパズルだな。 こいつを開けられるのは‥‥
English: That's right. An interesting puzzle, huh?

Japanese: ここに《錠》をセットした 人間だけ、なんだよ。
English: The person who set this lock is the only one who can open it.

Japanese: それは‥‥いったい、 だれなんですか！
English: Then who was it!?

Japanese: 被告人さ。
English: Simple. The accused.

Japanese: ‥‥あやめ‥‥さん？
English: What? Iris?

Japanese: 修験者の修行は、 この《修験洞》の奥で行われる。
English: When an acolyte undergoes training inside the Sacred Cavern...

Japanese: 立ち会う尼僧は、《からくり錠》で 修験洞の入り口を閉ざすんだ。
English: ...the attending sister is responsible for locking the entrance.

Japanese: 事件当夜も、そうだったはずだ。 綾里　真宵は、ここに入った。
English: Obviously, the night of the murder was no exception. Maya Fey was to train in there.

Japanese: （ビキニさんが言っていた‥‥）
English: (Sister Bikini did mention it a number of times, now that I think about it.)

Japanese: （修行の開始は、あやめさんに まかせた、って‥‥！）
English: (She said that Iris was left in charge of supervising the early stages of the training.)

Japanese: だから‥‥《錠》をはずせるのは、 被告人・あやめだけだ。
English: So Iris, the accused, is the only one who can open this lock.

Japanese: 今、彼女はこっちへ向かっている。 ‥‥オレが手配した。
English: I've arranged for her to be brought here now.

Japanese: （あやめさんにしか 開けられない《錠》か‥‥）
English: (Iris is the only person who can open this lock...?)

Japanese: （たのむ！　真宵ちゃん‥‥ ブジでいてくれよ！）
English: (I hope she gets here soon! ...Hang on in there, Maya. We'll get you out!)
It was two years ago...

Japanese: 綾里　千尋は、ある男を 追いかけていた。
English: Mia Fey was pursuing someone. A man.

Japanese: 彼女は、あまりに巨大な相手を 敵に回しちまったのさ。
English: But she bit off more than she could chew. She made a very dangerous enemy.

Japanese: （‥‥その事件なら、 ぼくも知っている‥‥）
English: (Yeah, that's one case I'll never forget.)

Japanese: でも‥‥そいつには、 ぼく自身が決着をつけたはずだ！
English: But... I got that guy! Personally!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥綾里　千尋を 殺害した犯人は、捕らえられたさ。
English: Sure. Ms. Fey's murderer was caught.

Japanese: しかし！　‥‥彼女はもう、 帰っては来ねえ。
English: But that won't bring her back!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それはそうだけど‥‥
English: W-Well, no... but...

Japanese: あのとき、彼女のそばにいたのは、 アンタだけだ。
English: You were with her at the time. You and no one else.

Japanese: アンタが‥‥彼女を 守ってやるべきだったのさ！
English: It was your responsibility! You should have protected her!

Japanese: 綾里　千尋が《師匠》だと‥‥？ 笑わせるな、まるほどう！
English: You say Mia Fey was your teacher! Well then, I'd say you've learned nothing, Trite!

Japanese: アンタは、彼女のイノチを奪った。 そして、今‥‥
English: You robbed her of her life. And now...

Japanese: 彼女の妹のイノチすら、 見殺しにしようとしているのさ！
English: ...you've let her sister suffer the same fate!

Japanese: （ぼ‥‥ぼくが‥‥ 真宵ちゃんを‥‥！）
English: (I... I haven't sentenced Maya to death...! No...!)

Japanese: あの。‥‥ちょっと 見てもらえますか？
English: Um, Godot... Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　オレの目は、水平線の かなたでカモメと遊んでいるさ。
English: Ha...! My eyes have a date with the horizon. They're flirting with the gulls.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士に協力する つもりはねえぜ。
English: I don't have any intention of aiding a defense attorney like you, Trite.

Japanese: 少しぐらい、 協力してあげなさい！
English: But you must help him! Even just a little!

Japanese: （きみが言うなよ‥‥）
English: (Look who's talking!)

Japanese: カッコよく断られるのが、これほど ハラ立たしいとは知らなかったわ。
English: I had no idea such a smooth refusal could make me so angry!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・中庭
English: February 9 Inner Temple Garden

Japanese: 何か、不自然な空気を 感じるわ‥‥なぜかしら。
English: There's a strange feeling in the air. I wonder what it is...

Japanese: （そ、それより‥‥）
English: (Strange feeling? Forget about that!)

Japanese: （なんだ？　あの灯ろうに 書いてある‥‥）
English: (What's that strange writing on the stone lantern?)

Japanese: ぎゃあああ‥‥ッス！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: なんで来ちゃったッスか！ ダメって言ったはずッス！
English: What are you doing here, sir!? I thought I said you can't come in here!

Japanese: ‥‥手がかりのあるところ、 狩魔の影、あり。
English: Don't you know, Scruffy? A Von Karma will always show up wherever there is a clue!

Japanese: ここには、 おもしろいコトがありそうね‥‥
English: And there are plenty of clues to be found here.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: こ、これ‥‥ いったい、なんですか！
English: What the heck is that!?

Japanese: ううう‥‥見つけちゃったッスか。
English: Uh-oh... You spotted it, huh, pal?

Japanese: “見つける”前に、 イヤでも目につきますよ！
English: Are you kidding me!? It couldn't be more obvious!

Japanese: ‥‥《マヨイ》‥‥ そう書かれているわね。
English: It says... "Maya"...

Japanese: その文字は‥‥ご想像どおり、 血で書かれているッス。
English: Yeah. And before you ask, it's written in blood...

Japanese: そのあたりのコト‥‥ 詳しく聞かせなさい、ヒゲッ！
English: You will answer all our questions now, Scruffy! Without exception!

Japanese: りょ‥‥ リョーカイッス！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥雪の中から、 《お守り》がのぞいている。
English: There's a charm or something poking out from the snow.

Japanese: （ずいぶん古いものだ。 革のヒモがついているな‥‥）
English: (It looks pretty old. There's a leather cord tied to it, too.)

Japanese: どうやら、被害者のもの らしいッス。
English: Apparently, it belonged to the victim.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスの‥‥？
English: To Ms. Elise Deauxnim?

Japanese: 被害者の死体のクビに、切断された 革のヒモが引っかかっていたッス。
English: Yeah. There was a broken leather cord around the lady's neck.

Japanese: その切り口が、その《お守り》の ヒモと、ピッタリ一致するッス。
English: The ends of the cords found on both the victim and on this charm match exactly.

Japanese: （こいつは‥‥）
English: (This means that this charm definitely belonged to Ms. Deauxnim.)

Japanese: （トンでもないシロモノ かもしれないな‥‥）
English: (This could be a really vital piece of evidence.)

Japanese: 証拠品《家元の護符》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Kurain Master's Talisman added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 小さな灯ろうに、 小さなししおどし‥‥
English: A little lantern, a little statue...

Japanese: かわいらしい庭園ね。 ‥‥おや。
English: It is certainly a quaint little garden. Oh!

Japanese: ‥‥この、大きな おちゃわんだけど‥‥
English: What is this huge bowl here for?

Japanese: あ。専門的には、 《ちょうずばち》ッス！
English: Oh, I know this one! It's... um... It's a ...water bowl, isn't it?

Japanese: 他のものはすべて 凍りついているのに‥‥
English: Everything else is completely frozen over...

Japanese: ちょうずばちの氷だけ、 キレイに割られているわ！
English: But the ice in this bowl has been broken.

Japanese: 氷が‥‥ （何か、イミがあるのか‥‥？）
English: That's true... (I wonder if this has any impact on the case...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あの。 それは、自分が‥‥
English: Umm... Well... I, uh...

Japanese: カオを洗ったッス。 ちょっと、眠かったッス。
English: I doused my face earlier with the water in this. I was getting kinda sleepy...

Japanese: ぎゃあああッ！
English: Gaaargh!

Japanese: これからは、眠くなったら 私のところへ来るコトね！
English: Next time you're sleepy, you need only come to me! Got it!?

Japanese: “かがり火”だ。
English: There are a couple of torches here.

Japanese: 街灯のない山の中だから、 大切な明かりなのだろう。
English: I guess you need all the light you can get up here in the mountains.

Japanese: その、かがり火ッスけど‥‥
English: Yeah. I was looking at those things, pal.

Japanese: 最近、燃やされた跡があるッス。
English: It looks like they've been lit pretty recently.
Recently? How recent are we talking about?

Japanese: おそらくは、事件当夜と 考えられているッス。
English: As recent as the night of the murder...

Japanese: （事件当夜は、ここで 真宵ちゃんの修行があった）
English: (Maya was training here on the night of the murder...)

Japanese: （かがり火を燃やしても、 不自然ではないだろうな‥‥）
English: (It's not unreasonable to think these things were lit up at the time, I guess.)

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、これ。 ここだけ、雪がない。
English: ...Well, this is odd. There's no snow on the ground here.

Japanese: しかも‥‥不自然なほど キッチリ、長方形に。
English: And it's an almost perfect rectangle. It couldn't have happened naturally.

Japanese: だれかが、キレイに雪を 持ち去ったようね。
English: Yes. It appears as though someone has carefully cleared away the snow.

Japanese: この、ヒゲがッ！
English: No doubt it was you, wasn't it, Scruffy!?

Japanese: じじ、自分じゃないッス！
English: Huh? No, it wasn't me, sir!

Japanese: ワレワレ警察が来たときは、 すでにこんな感じだったッス。
English: It was already like that when my men and I got here to start the investigation.

Japanese: （じゃあ‥‥ いったい、だれが‥‥？）
English: (If it wasn't the police, then who did it...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうだ！ ここだけのハナシがあるッス。
English: Oh, yeah. There's something I need to tell you. Just between the three of us, OK?

Japanese: 何かしら？
English: What is it?

Japanese: これは、ゴクヒッスよ。 ダレにも言っちゃダメッス。
English: It's top secret. You can't tell anyone, got it?

Japanese: ‥‥いいわ。
English: ...Understood.

Japanese: 小さいころ、近所のスモウ大会で 負けたときのこと‥‥
English: Well, when I was a kid, I got knocked out of the local wrestling contest, you see.

Japanese: くやしくて、泣きながらドヒョウの 砂を持って帰ったッス。
English: I was so disappointed, I just picked up my mouth guard and cried all the way home.

Japanese: そいつを台所にぶちまけて、 しこたま怒られたッス。
English: Man, I got in so much trouble when I left it on the couch!

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ それで終わり？
English: ...Is that it?

Japanese: 終わりッス。
English: That's it.

Japanese: どひょおッ！
English: Gaaargh!

Japanese: （だれかが、雪を持ち去った‥‥ 何か、イミがあるんだろうな）
English: (Whoever cleared the snow away must have had a reason...)

Japanese: （今は、見当もつかないけど）
English: (Although I can't even begin to imagine what that reason could be.)

Japanese: それで、どうなんですか？ 奥の院の捜査は‥‥
English: So how's the Inner Temple investigation going...?

Japanese: わが国がほこる、最新の 科学捜査でがんばったッス！
English: We have the best scientific forensic team in the world working the place!

Japanese: 科学捜査、ね‥‥。 それで、その結果は？
English: Forensic team? And what was the outcome of this scientific study?

Japanese: とりあえず、 科学的に異常なしッス！
English: Nothing! Scientifically speaking, the place is as clean as a whistle.

Japanese: それですむと ホンキで思ってるのかしら？
English: That's simply not good enough, Scruffy! Do you think I'll let you get away with that?

Japanese: ううううう‥‥
English: Uh-oh...

Japanese: パッと見た感じ、気になる ところが２点ほど、あったッス。
English: Well, there were a couple of things that bothered me when I first got here, sir...

Japanese: あの灯ろうと、雪のない地面ね。
English: That stone lantern, and the surrounding area that's not covered in snow, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥科学的に、ちょっと アヤシイ感じがするッス。
English: They looked kinda fishy to me. You know, scientifically speaking.

Japanese: それで、鑑識課の連中に 調べてもらったッスが‥‥
English: So I got the lab boys to look them over.

Japanese: その結果は‥‥ちょっと、 言いにくいモノになったッス。
English: The results were... It's kinda hard to say, actually.

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、 ぼくも調べてみるか‥‥）
English: (I guess I better look into them myself.)

Japanese: あの‥‥真宵ちゃんのこと ですけど‥‥
English: Um, about Maya...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 ゴドー検事から聞いたッスか？
English: Oh... Um... Did Mr. Godot tell you yet?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ、まあ。
English: ...Yes, we heard.

Japanese: 警察とは別に、捜索隊が 動いているッスけど‥‥
English: He's got a search party out looking for her.

Japanese: おぼろ橋のこっち側は、 姿をかくせる場所がないッス。
English: But there's nowhere else she could have disappeared to on this side of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: ワレワレとしては、御剣検事の 到着を待つしかないッス。
English: All we can do is wait for Mr. Edgeworth to get here, pal.

Japanese: 御剣の‥‥‥
English: Edgeworth?

Japanese: 被告人・あやめを護送して こちらへ向かっているッス。
English: He's escorting Iris, the defendant, over here.

Japanese: 修験洞の《錠》を開けられるのは 彼女だけ。‥‥待つしかないわ。
English: We'll have to wait, since she is the only one who can open the door to the Sacred Cavern.

Japanese: そういえば、御剣検事がアンタに 話したいコトがあるらしいッス。
English: Oh! I think Mr. Edgeworth wants to talk to you about something too, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣が、ぼくに？ なんだろう‥‥
English: ...Huh? I wonder what it is...

Japanese: あ。くれぐれも、いいニュースを キタイしちゃ、ダメッスよ。
English: Just don't be expecting any good news when you talk to him.

Japanese: 御剣検事の声、死にたくなるほど 暗かったッスから。
English: When I spoke to him, Mr. Edgeworth sounded so down, I felt like I was drowning...

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Sounds... pleasant...)

Japanese: この《マヨイ》という文字‥‥ 血、で書かれてるんですか？
English: "Maya"... So these letters are written in blood?

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。しかも‥‥ 被害者の血、らしいッス。
English: Yeah... What's worse is it's the victim's blood.

Japanese: なんですって！ エリス先生の血‥‥
English: Huh!? This is Ms. Deauxnim's blood?

Japanese: 精密検査は、まだッスが‥‥ おそらく、まちがいないッス。
English: We haven't done a detailed analysis yet, but... it's looking that way, pal.

Japanese: おもしろいわね‥‥
English: Hmm... Most interesting...

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 あなたにも、わかるわね？
English: Phoenix Wright. I presume you know, don't you.

Japanese: この文字が‥‥なぜ、 逆さまに書かれているか？
English: Why Maya's name is written upside-down.

Japanese: そ、それは自分も フシギに思っていたッス！
English: You know what, that was really bothering me, too.

Japanese: 独自の調査をもとに、さっき！ 自分なりのコタエを出したッス。
English: But I just came up with the answer. The result of my own special Gumshoe Investigation!

Japanese: ‥‥そう。言ってごらんなさい。
English: Really? Do tell...

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥
English: Well, on the night of the murder...

Japanese: あの灯ろうは‥‥、きっと 逆さまに立っていたッス！
English: ...that stone lantern was upside-down!

Japanese: とにかく。この状況で 考えられることは、１つね。
English: Ahem! Anyway, there's really only one logical explanation to this mystery.

Japanese: この文字は‥‥自分の血で、 天流斎　エリス自身が書いたのよ。
English: Ms. Elise Deauxnim wrote these letters herself, in her own blood.

Japanese: う‥‥ウソだ！ （そんなこと‥‥あり得ない！）
English: Y-You must be joking! (That's impossible!)

Japanese: ‥‥被害者の血で書かれた文字、 その他の遺留品から考えて‥‥
English: From the writing in the victim's blood and the other clues left behind...

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスの殺害現場は、 この、奥の院の中庭だった‥‥
English: ...it seems pretty likely that this garden was the scene of Ms. Elise Deauxnim's murder.

Japanese: 現在、ワレワレは そう考えているッス。
English: At least, that's our current theory on how the events took place, pal.

Japanese: えええええええッ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生が‥‥ ここで殺害された‥‥？
English: So Ms. Deauxnim was killed here...?

Japanese: そうとしか考えられない、と みんな言ってるッス。
English: However you wanna look at it, that's what people are saying now.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ！　ビキニさんが 葉桜院の境内で目撃した‥‥
English: Then... what Sister Bikini saw in the courtyard at the main temple was...

Japanese: あの《犯行現場》は どうなるんですか！
English: Well, what was it!? If that wasn't the scene of the crime, what did she see?

Japanese: どうなるッスか？　狩魔検事。 ぎゃあああッ！
English: What do you think, Ms. von Karmaaaaa!?

Japanese: ‥‥独自の調査をもとに、 好きなコタエを出しなさい。
English: Surely a special Gumshoe Investigation would reveal the answer, no?

Japanese: ‥‥でも！　遺留品だって、 灯ろうの血文字だって‥‥
English: But wait a sec. The bloody writing and all the other clues here...

Japanese: 事件のあと、だれかが 細工したかもしれないでしょう！
English: Someone could've easily set all that up after the murder had taken place, right?

Japanese: それは‥‥あり得ないッス。
English: Sorry, but that's not an option, pal.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか！
English: Why not!?

Japanese: ‥‥忘れたの？　成歩堂　龍一。
English: How quickly you forget, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 事件当夜から、さっきまで‥‥ ここへ来ることは不可能だったの。
English: Ever since the incident occurred, this place has been completely inaccessible...

Japanese: なぜなら‥‥ おぼろ橋は、なかったのだから！
English: ...because Dusky Bridge was completely burned out.

Japanese: あの橋の修理には、 自分が立ち会ったッス。
English: I was overseeing repairs to the bridge the whole time.

Japanese: 警察より先に、ここへ来た者は いないッス。つまり‥‥
English: No one came over here before me and my men.

Japanese: この中庭に、遺留品を持ちこむのは ゼッタイ不可能、というコトッス！
English: Which means no one could've planted all this stuff here, pal.

Japanese: うう‥‥ッ！ （は‥‥反論できない‥‥）
English: Nngh...! (I-I can't think of a counterargument to that...)

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥
English: I knew it...

Japanese: 灯ろうの血文字を見つけたとき、 水洗いしておけばよかったッス‥‥
English: I should've washed the blood off the lantern as soon as I got here.

Japanese: （もし、本当の殺人現場が ここだったのならば‥‥）
English: (If this really was the scene of the crime...)

Japanese: （ビキニさんの見た《事件》‥‥ カクニンしたほうがいい！）
English: (...then I need to figure out exactly what it was that Bikini actually saw!)

Japanese: ナゾのミステリーに 包まれているッス。
English: That guy's got more mysteries than a Choose Your Own Escapade book, pal.

Japanese: それはもう聞きましたよ。
English: Yeah, I know. Trust me I already know.

Japanese: まったく‥‥今日も 法廷を無断ですっぽかして。
English: I can't believe he skipped the trial like that, without even getting permission.

Japanese: これがフツウの会社なら、 クビッスよ！
English: He'd have been fired for sure if he worked for a normal company.

Japanese: （なんだなんだ。検事局は 自由な社風がウリなのか？）
English: (Hmm... Never would've expected the Prosecutor's Office to be so lax...)

Japanese: そんなことより、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Don't get the wrong impression, Phoenix Wright. More importantly...

Japanese: 刑事たちは、あのオトコの 身体のコト、知らないようね。
English: Doesn't it strike you as odd that the police are unaware of that man's medical situation?

Japanese: 死人が地獄からよみがえるには‥‥ くだらねえ手つづきが必要なのさ。
English: Of course, being extradited from Hell is a tedious affair.

Japanese: 定期的な身体機能の精密検査、 体内物質の補給・調整‥‥
English: The meticulous regeneration and adjustment of all your internal organs is...

Japanese: めんどくせえ現代医学にすがって、 みっともなく生きながらえる‥‥
English: Well... Let's just say modern medicine allows us all to live to a ripe old age.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、今の、このオレだ。
English: Even someone like me.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。どうして、 ヒミツにしているんだろう‥‥？
English: Yeah. Why is he hiding that from everyone?

Japanese: ‥‥もうひとつ、 妙なコトがあるわ。
English: Additionally it raises another question...

Japanese: そんなヒミツを、どうして あなたにだけは、話したのかしら？
English: Why did he choose to tell only you?

Japanese: （あ。たしかに‥‥）
English: (Yeah. Why did he?)

Japanese: ムカシ‥‥一度だけ、 尋問を受けたことがあるッス。
English: She cross-examined me once, you know.

Japanese: あれは‥‥名勝負だったッス。
English: Yeah. That was us. The big guns locking horns.

Japanese: 証人対弁護士！　アツいアツい カケヒキが展開されたッス。
English: Witness versus lawyer! It was a battle of cunning tactics and tricks.

Japanese: （証人がカケヒキしちゃ マズいだろ‥‥）
English: (But the witness isn't supposed to play any tricks...)

Japanese: おしいヒトを亡くしたッス‥‥。
English: I can't believe she's gone...

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、行方不明ッス。
English: The situation hasn't changed. We still don't know anything about Maya's whereabouts.

Japanese: もう、修験洞の中にいるとしか 考えられないッス。
English: She's gotta be in the Sacred Cavern, though. That's the only place left.

Japanese: かなりサムいらしいッスからね。 早く助けないと、アブナイッス。
English: I heard it's real cold in there. We've gotta get her out fast, or it could be bad, pal.

Japanese: なんとか、できないんですか！
English: Isn't there anything you can do, Detective!?

Japanese: アンタに、そんなコトを 言われるスジアイはないッス！
English: What are you talking about!?
...Huh?

Japanese: ワレワレの対応は、これ以上 考えられないほど、素早いッス！
English: We're already on it! You couldn't get a faster response from a computer.

Japanese: ゴドー検事が、ものすごい速さで 手配してくれたからッス！
English: Mr. Godot put everything in place right away. That guy doesn't mess around.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事が‥‥？
English: G-Godot?

Japanese: ナゾのミステリーに包まれた コーヒー好きのチコク魔ッスが‥‥
English: Yeah. The coffee-drinking mystery man who didn't show in court.

Japanese: 今回のことで、自分はちょっぴり あのヒトを見直したッス。
English: I gotta say. He's totally made me rethink my perception of him, pal.

Japanese: おッ！　そうだ。 聞いてくれるッスか！
English: Hey, you wanna hear something, pal? Come on, I know you do!

Japanese: 今度、マコクンが自分に、コートを プレゼントしてくれるッス！
English: Maggey says she's gonna buy me a new coat!

Japanese: へえ！　よかったですね。
English: Really? That's great!

Japanese: こないだの事件の お礼だそうッス！
English: Yeah. I think it's to say thanks for helping her out during that last case!

Japanese: 自分のエキスがしみこんだ、 このカツオ風味のコート‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: これでやっと、 ヤッカイ払いできるッス！
English: I can finally chuck out this stinky, sweat-soaked rag!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。そのコート、それほど 大切なものではなかったの。
English: You mean, you don't have any special attachments to that coat...?

Japanese: 去年アメリカから、せっかく 送り返してやったのに。
English: And after all the effort I took to mail it back to you from Germany...

Japanese: これはこれで、 ダイジに取っておくッス。
English: Ah, well, I'll be sure to take good care of it once it's retired, sir.

Japanese: 事件のあった夜から、こっちに 閉じこめられていたらしいッス。
English: Apparently, the girl was trapped over here since the night of the murder.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのこと‥‥ 何か言ってませんでしたか？
English: Did she say anything to you about Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには、 話してくれなかったんです‥‥
English: She wouldn't tell me...

Japanese: アンタに話さないことを、自分に 教えてくれるワケ、ないッス。
English: She's not gonna tell me something if she wouldn't even speak to you, pal.

Japanese: 泣いているばっかりで、 見ていてツラかったッス。
English: She was real upset. She wouldn't stop crying.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一！　ぼやぼやしないで 早く助けてあげなさい！
English: Phoenix Wright! Look sharp and go comfort that poor young girl!

Japanese: わ、わかってるよ‥‥
English: I will... Trust me, I will...

Japanese: やさしくて、いい おばちゃんッス。
English: She's a sweet old lady.

Japanese: とても、ウソをつくような ヒトには見えないッス。
English: She doesn't look like the lying type to me.

Japanese: でも‥‥ビキニさんの証言には 問題があったみたいですね。
English: But there was a problem with her testimony, wasn't there?

Japanese: あれはきっと、おばちゃんの せいじゃないッス。
English: Yeah, but I don't think it was her fault.

Japanese: ‥‥自分は正しいコトを言ってる つもりが、そうでもなかった‥‥
English: She was just trying to say the right thing, and it came out wrong, that's all.

Japanese: 自分には、わかるッス！ よくあるコトッス！
English: I know what it's like. Happens to me all the time!

Japanese: ‥‥キサマは、刑事のクセに 観察力がなさすぎるの！
English: For a detective, you are completely lacking in powers of observation!

Japanese: どうにも、ヒミツの多い 被害者ッス。
English: She sure was a mysterious person.

Japanese: 本名はおろか、今までの 経歴も、完全に不明ッス。
English: Her real name, her past... It's all a complete mystery.

Japanese: 妙な話ね‥‥。
English: Still, it strikes me as odd...

Japanese: 身分を知られたくないのなら、 作家になど、ならないはずよ。
English: Why would someone who wishes to remain anonymous become an author?

Japanese: きっと、あれッスね。
English: You know what probably happened?

Japanese: こんなに売れっ子になるとは 思わなかったッスよ。
English: I bet she didn't know her books were gonna be such a success. That's what I think.

Japanese: ‥‥自分には、自信が なくなってきたッス。
English: I dunno. I'm not so sure anymore.

Japanese: 今日、発見された手がかりで 事件はガラッと変わったッス。
English: The clues we found today have turned this case all topsy-turvy.

Japanese: 正直、彼女が犯人だとは、 考えにくい状況ッス。
English: I can't see how Iris could've done it now.

Japanese: ‥‥それはどうかしらね、ヒゲ。 私は、まったく逆だと思うわ！
English: Oh? How interesting, Scruffy. Sadly, I don't share the same sentiment.

Japanese: 明日は、カクゴすることね、 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Prepare yourself well for tomorrow, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （この検事さんも シゴト熱心だよな‥‥）
English: (Why does every prosecutor want to burn me at the stake so badly...?)

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生の お弟子さんだそうッス。
English: He was Ms. Elise Deauxnim's apprentice, or so I heard.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。
English: ...Well, I suppose you could call him that.

Japanese: 似顔絵、描いてもらったッス。
English: The guy drew a portrait of me, you know.

Japanese: 最高じゃないですか。
English: Well, lucky you, I suppose.

Japanese: でも。５０円、とられたッス。
English: Yeah, but I had to pay him 50 cents for it.

Japanese: 最低ですね。
English: That's pretty awful.

Japanese: どうも、気のないそぶりッスね、 アンタも。
English: You aren't really enthused about this guy either, huh...?

Japanese: 今日の裁判も、メチャクチャに してくれたそうですから。
English: Well... he made a mockery of the court today.

Japanese: （たまに、ワザとやってるんじゃ ないかと思うときがあるよ）
English: (And I'm almost sure he did it on purpose, too...)

Japanese: いやー、ベッピンさんッス。
English: Wow! What a beauty, huh?

Japanese: ぎゃああッス！
English: Gaargh!

Japanese: 最初の“間”は何かしら、ヒゲ！
English: What was that long pause for, Scruffy!?

Japanese: いやー、ステキなブローチッス。
English: That's a real nice broach she's got, too.

Japanese: ぎゃああッス！
English: Gaargh!

Japanese: （‥‥さっきから、ホメかたを まちがっているような‥‥）
English: (Gumshoe needs to learn how to give compliments faster than Franziska gives lashes.)

Japanese: もお、ひさしぶりッス。 １年ぶりぐらいッスかねー。
English: I haven't seen Mr. Edgeworth for about a year. Talk about a long time!

Japanese: アイツ、わざわざ外国で 何やってるんですか？
English: Do you know why he moved overseas?

Japanese: さあ。カッコつけてるんじゃ ないッスか。やっぱり。
English: I don't know. Probably to look cool.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ハクがつくッスからねー。 海外で研究なんて。
English: There's a lot of weight attached to doing research overseas, you know.

Japanese: 自分も、パリで修行してきたら、 ムッシュ・イトノコギリッスよ。
English: Once I've done my stint in Paris, you'll have to call me Monsieur Gumchaussure.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ、ブカに完全に リカイされていないな‥‥）
English: People really don't get Edgeworth, do they?

Japanese: ‥‥説明しないほうが悪いのよ。
English: It's his own fault for not explaining anything about himself.

Japanese: ほら。弁護士のバッジです。
English: Hey, look. It's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: はっはっ。あいかわらず サエないバッジッスね。
English: Ha ha. Good one, pal. You're always good for a laugh with your little badge.

Japanese: やっぱり男は、 ダマって警察手帳ッス。
English: But a real man... A real man has a police badge.

Japanese: お。キレイッス！　もしかして、 キャンデーッスか？
English: Hey, that looks good. Is it a cough drop?

Japanese: ちょうどノドが痛かったッス。
English: I tell you, pal. I've had a sore throat all day.

Japanese: ちち、ちがいますよ！ 勾玉です、勾玉！
English: Ack! No! That's my Magatama! You can't eat it!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。ちがうッスか。
English: Oh...

Japanese: （いきなり口に放りこむ クセは、なおったらしいな）
English: (At least he doesn't just put stuff in his mouth first and ask questions later anymore.)

Japanese: この掛け軸なんですけど‥‥ 何か知ってますか？
English: Do you know anything about this scroll, Detective?

Japanese: お。うまそうな ニオイがするッス！
English: Well, I know it smells great!

Japanese: いやー、こんな日は やっぱり、カレーッスねえ。
English: Yeah, this is just the kind of weather for some steaming mashed potatoes and gravy.

Japanese: 自分、大のカレー好きッスから、 このコートにも、ニオイがシミ
English: I really love that stuff. I eat it so much that my coat even smel--

Japanese: ぎゃあああッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: カレー以外のコメントは ないのかしら？
English: Have you nothing else to say about this than your inane ramblings about gravy!?

Japanese: 特に、異常なしッス。
English: N-Not really.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいです。
English: ...Let's leave it at that, OK?

Japanese: 被害者が身につけていたと 考えて、まちがいないッス！
English: That thing must've been worn by the victim. There's no other way of looking at it.

Japanese: 被害者のクビに、革のヒモが 引っかかっていた、とか。
English: You mean, because of the matching leather cord found around the victim's neck?

Japanese: そッス。きっと、いつも 身につけていたッスね。
English: Yeah. I bet it was one of those things she always wore.

Japanese: 自分も去年から、交通安全の 《お守り》を持ってるッス。
English: I've got this safe driving charm I've been wearing for over a year now.

Japanese: これで自分が事故にあったら、 アイツのせいッス。
English: If I ever get into an accident, it guarantees it'll be the other guy's fault.

Japanese: （そういうものじゃ ないだろう、《お守り》って）
English: (Isn't it supposed to stop you from having an accident in the first place?)

Japanese: 自分には、ムズカシイことは よくわからないッスが‥‥
English: I didn't follow the ins and outs of it, but...

Japanese: ケッキョク、これは キョーキじゃなかったッスね？
English: ...it turned out that this wasn't the murder weapon, right?

Japanese: ビキニさんが目撃したのが 正しければ‥‥ですけど。
English: If what Sister Bikini saw is right, then no, it's not.

Japanese: 凶器が、刃の根本まで身体に 刺さったのなら‥‥
English: She said the murder weapon was thrust into the victim up to the hilt.

Japanese: 血痕の残りかたが 不自然ですからね。このカタナは。
English: But the blood stains on this aren't consistent with her story.

Japanese: 大丈夫ッス！　きっと自分が、 キョーキを見つけてみせるッス！
English: Don't worry, pal! I'll uncover the real murder weapon.

Japanese: 春が来るまでにッ！
English: If I haven't turned it up by Spring, you can take my badge!

Japanese: （待てないよ！）
English: (The case will be long over by then, Gumshoe!)

Japanese: 被害者が持っていたツエ、ッス。
English: That staff belonged to the victim.

Japanese: 水晶がなくなっている以外、 アヤシイところ、ありませんね。
English: Yeah, and apart from the crystal missing from the top, there's nothing wrong with it.

Japanese: あ、そうだ！ ここだけのハナシがあるッス。
English: Oh, I just remembered something important! This is just between us, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥事件にカンケイ あることかしら？
English: Is it going to have anything to do with the case, Detective?

Japanese: いや。そうでもないッスけど‥‥
English: Well, no. But it's really...

Japanese: じゃ、いいわ。
English: Then I'm not interested.

Japanese: うううう。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 この、現場写真ですけど‥‥
English: What do you make of this photo of the crime scene...?

Japanese: ホント、ヒドい犯人ッスねー。
English: It's really gruesome... All I gotta say is that the killer is messed up in the head.

Japanese: でも、キョーキがこの 七支刀じゃないなら‥‥
English: But it turns out that Shichishito thing wasn't even the murder weapon.

Japanese: どうして、こんなコトを したッスかねえ。
English: So then, why would someone stab it in the victim like that?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥それはナゾだな）
English: (That's the mystery we're here to solve...)

Japanese: 今日の法廷は、もう お祭り騒ぎだったッス。
English: It was like a circus today in court.

Japanese: ‥‥このラクガキのせいで。
English: All thanks to this little sketch.

Japanese: 何度も言うようだけど‥‥
English: At the risk of repeating myself...

Japanese: トモダチは選びなさい。 成歩堂　龍一。
English: ...you really ought to choose your friends more carefully, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、矢張は あんなオトコだけど‥‥
English: OK, I know Larry's a bit out there...

Japanese: あやめさんを助けようとする キモチは、ホンモノだと思う。
English: ...but he genuinely wants to help Iris. I'm sure of that.

Japanese: イミもなく、こんなものを 描くようなヤツじゃないんだ。
English: He wouldn't have produced this for no good reason.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。ホントに お人よしね‥‥あなたたち。
English: You are nothing more than a marshmallow on the inside. The both of you.

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋の近くで 見つかったんですよね？
English: This was found close to Dusky Bridge, wasn't it?

Japanese: あの絵描きは、 そう言っているッス。
English: That's what that artist guy said, yeah.

Japanese: 被告人が、燃えるおぼろ橋を ピョーイと飛びこえたあと、
English: Right after the defendant flew over the burning bridge!

Japanese: 着地したときに落とした、とか。
English: According to his testimony, she dropped it when she landed on the other side.

Japanese: （でも‥‥この水晶は、 あやめさんのものじゃない）
English: (But this crystal didn't belong to Iris.)

Japanese: そう。それは、天流斎　エリスの ツエから落ちたものよ。
English: The crystal had dropped out of Ms. Elise Deauxnim's staff.

Japanese: しかも、水晶には 血痕が付着しているッス。
English: And there are traces of blood on it, too.

Japanese: （そんなものが‥‥なぜ！ おぼろ橋のたもとに‥‥）
English: (The real question is what was it doing near the foot of Dusky Bridge...?)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 ‥‥これなんですけど。
English: What about this, Detective Gumshoe? Any thoughts?

Japanese: うーん‥‥そッスねー‥‥。
English: Yeah... Well...

Japanese: ちょっと今日はサムいッスから、 アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: It's pretty chilly today, huh? My brain doesn't work properly in the cold.

Japanese: 夏になるまで待つかしら？ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Perhaps we'll just have to wait until Summer when it's warmer?

Japanese: あ。自分、アツくても アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: Oh, no. My brain doesn't work properly in the heat.

Japanese: なにぶん、ナイーブッスから。
English: I'm kinda weak in the head.

Japanese: （自分で言うなよ‥‥）
English: (There are some things you shouldn't advertise, Gumshoe. Especially in front of her...)
